snippets.title: Derived ratio and domain flag
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "price": [250000, 410000, 180000],
    "sqft": [1200, 2100, 900],
    "plan": ["basic", "enterprise", "basic"],
})

df = df.assign(
    price_per_sqft=df["price"] / df["sqft"].clip(lower=1),
    is_enterprise=(df["plan"] == "enterprise").astype(int),
    log_price=np.log1p(df["price"]),
)
print(df)
snippets.explanation: Ratios need safe denominators; log1p compresses skewed price tails; boolean flags become 0/1 columns models consume directly.

snippets.title: Interaction column for linear models
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "tenure_months": [3, 24, 60],
    "monthly_spend": [10, 45, 120],
})

df["spend_x_tenure"] = df["monthly_spend"] * df["tenure_months"]
# Captures that high spend matters more for long-tenured customers in a linear model
snippets.explanation: Multiply columns when domain logic says one feature's effect scales with another; avoid blind all-pairs products on high-dimensional data.

snippets.title: Datetime parts and cyclical hour encoding
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.DataFrame({"event_time": ["2024-06-15 23:30:00", "2024-06-16 08:00:00"]})
ts = pd.to_datetime(df["event_time"])

df = df.assign(
    hour=ts.dt.hour,
    dayofweek=ts.dt.dayofweek,
    is_weekend=(ts.dt.dayofweek >= 5).astype(int),
    hour_sin=np.sin(2 * np.pi * ts.dt.hour / 24),
    hour_cos=np.cos(2 * np.pi * ts.dt.hour / 24),
)
print(df)
snippets.explanation: Extract calendar fields with `.dt`; sin/cos pairs treat hour 23 as close to hour 0 for models that assume smooth numeric distance.

snippets.title: Customer aggregates with groupby join
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd

customers = pd.DataFrame({"customer_id": [1, 2, 3]})
orders = pd.DataFrame({
    "customer_id": [1, 1, 2],
    "amount": [40, 60, 25],
})

agg = orders.groupby("customer_id", as_index=False).agg(
    order_count=("amount", "count"),
    total_spend=("amount", "sum"),
)

features = customers.merge(agg, on="customer_id", how="left").fillna(0)
print(features)
snippets.explanation: Groupby-aggregate-join is the standard pattern for row-level derived history features at a coarser grain.

snippets.title: Binning continuous age into ordinal buckets
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({"age": [16, 34, 72]})

df["age_bucket"] = pd.cut(
    df["age"],
    bins=[0, 18, 65, 120],
    labels=["minor", "adult", "senior"],
    include_lowest=True,
)
print(df)
snippets.explanation: Binning exposes threshold effects; downstream encoding (see Encoding) turns labels into model-ready numeric columns.
